definition: Migrates databases to AWS quickly and securely. Source database remains fully operational during the migration; supports homogeneous (e.g. Oracle to Oracle) and heterogeneous migrations (e.g. Oracle to Aurora MySQL) and continuous replication for change-data-capture.
Converts a source database schema and a majority of the custom code (stored procedures, views, etc.) to a format compatible with the target AWS database. Pairs with DMS to handle the data movement.

Lift-and-shift solution for rehosting any on-premises or other-cloud server onto EC2. Continuously replicates source server disks to a Staging Area Subnet, then performs cut-over with minimal downtime.
Already documented; duplicate skipped at merge time.
Already documented; duplicate skipped at merge time.

A managed cloud service that lets connected devices easily and securely interact with cloud applications and other devices. Supports MQTT, HTTP, and WebSockets; provides device registry, device shadows, and rules-engine routing to other AWS services.

Open-source edge runtime and cloud service for building, deploying, and managing intelligent device software. Lets devices run Lambda functions and ML inference locally, sync state, and operate while offline.

A fully managed service that automates each step required to analyse data from IoT devices. Filters, transforms, and enriches data before storing it in a time-series data store, then runs SQL queries or hosts notebooks for analysis.
Already documented; duplicate skipped at merge time.

A secure, fully managed content creation, storage, and collaboration service. Lets users create, edit, and share content stored in the cloud with built-in version control, comments, and per-folder permissions.
A secure, managed business email and calendar service with support for existing desktop and mobile clients. Compatible with Microsoft Outlook, Apple Mail, native Android/iOS apps, and IMAP/SMTP.
